heroDescription: A platform for marketers shaping the industry to share honest insights beyond brand narratives

mission.quote:
We see how often talents and hard work can be unseen,
and our goal is to remind who actually make
the vision happen.

paragraphs: 12 UNDER 12 is a collaborative project by Zorka.Agency and Influencer Marketing Hub, created to spotlight the people behind the industry. As Zorka.Agency marks 12 years in influencer marketing, we chose to celebrate this milestone by giving the stage to others. The concept is simple: 12 months, 12 marketers who have made a real impact on the industry.

We launched this project because influencer marketing still lacks platforms where professionals can openly share their honest experiences, challenges, and perspectives beyond brand narratives. Too often, the focus is on campaigns and results — not on the people making it all happen.
12 UNDER 12 changes that by creating a space for marketers to speak openly, share real insights, and shape the conversation around the industry today.

marketers.name: Alyona
marketers.intro: Alyona builds influencer programs that balance long-term brand reputation with measurable business outcomes across global and local markets.
marketers.bio:
I came up through performance marketing, but I crossed into influencer work the moment it sank in that trust is built in comments and DMs—not only in paid placements. Since then, I've split my time between scrappy teams and big brands, always chasing the same thing: programs that compound instead of resetting every quarter.

For this series I'm opening the notebook—early mistakes, what changed once we actually listened to creators, and the frameworks that keep partnerships from unraveling after the first brief.

sections.title: Biggest challenge
sections.content: One of the biggest challenges was proving the value of influencer marketing to teams that only looked at short-term CPA. We had to redesign reporting and show full-funnel impact.

sections.title: What changed in 2025
sections.content: Brands became more selective and started prioritizing sustainable creator relationships instead of one-off integrations.

sections.title: Advice to newcomers
sections.content: Start with the audience and their real behavior. A campaign is successful when the story feels natural to both the creator and the community.

sections.title: Future outlook
sections.content: The next step is deeper creator co-creation where influencers are involved earlier in product and communication decisions.

storyTopics.title: What makes your days easier—and what makes them harder?
storyTopics.content: When the brief is tight, legal moves, and creators answer on time, the week almost runs itself. The grind is everything else: edits that land at midnight, five tools that don't talk, and the meeting where someone asks for a "small" tweak that torches the posting window. Half of this job is strategy; the other half is air-traffic control.

storyTopics.title: The best campaign you ever ran—what made it work?
storyTopics.content: Not the one that blew up once. A year-long deal with a handful of creators who actually lived with the product, argued with us when something felt off, and stayed in the loop as we iterated. Revenue climbed without a spike; the comments read like real life. That's when I stopped chasing hero metrics on a slide deck.

storyTopics.title: Why is "influencer" inching toward "ambassador"?
storyTopics.content: One-off integrations exhaust everyone. Brands want people who show up in CRM, at events, in lifecycle emails—relationships that behave more like ambassadors than ad units. Contracts get longer, briefs get heavier, accountability gets clearer. It's influencer marketing growing into something closer to community and CRM.

storyTopics.title: Do people still trust influencers?
storyTopics.content: Trust didn't vanish—it got picky. Audiences reward the messy truth: how a product fits a real week, what still doesn't work, where the money comes from. They punish the polished script and the deal that never gets disclosed. Our job is to put brands next to voices who can stay human—and to label every paid line.

qaCards.question: Why marketing?
qaCards.answer: Because it combines analytics, psychology, and creativity in one discipline.

qaCards.question: Why this project?
qaCards.answer: It gives the industry practical stories from people doing the work every day.

qaCards.question: One principle you follow
qaCards.answer: Consistency beats virality when building long-term brand equity.

marketers.qaCardsStoryAfterTitle: What makes your days easier—and what makes them harder?

closingCoda.prompt:
Every time we will ask a question, that will wrap up your experience, and maybe the state of a whole industry.

So, what is the power of Influencer marketing?
